17: package com.pinterest.singer.loggingaudit.client;
18: 
19: import com.pinterest.singer.loggingaudit.client.common.LoggingAuditClientMetrics;
20: import com.pinterest.singer.loggingaudit.thrift.LoggingAuditHeaders;
21: import com.pinterest.singer.loggingaudit.thrift.LoggingAuditEvent;
22: import com.pinterest.singer.loggingaudit.thrift.LoggingAuditStage;
23: import com.pinterest.singer.loggingaudit.thrift.configuration.KafkaSenderConfig;
24: import com.pinterest.singer.metrics.OpenTsdbMetricConverter;
25: import com.pinterest.singer.utils.CommonUtils;
26: 
27: import org.apache.kafka.clients.producer.Callback;
28: import org.apache.kafka.clients.producer.KafkaProducer;
29: import org.apache.kafka.clients.producer.ProducerRecord;
30: import org.apache.kafka.clients.producer.RecordMetadata;
31: import org.apache.kafka.common.PartitionInfo;
32: import org.apache.thrift.TException;
33: import org.apache.thrift.TSerializer;
34: import org.slf4j.Logger;
35: import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory;
36: 
37: import java.util.ArrayList;
38: import java.util.List;
39: import java.util.Map;
40: import java.util.Set;
41: import java.util.concurrent.ConcurrentHashMap;
42: import java.util.concurrent.LinkedBlockingDeque;
43: import java.util.concurrent.ThreadLocalRandom;
44: import java.util.concurrent.TimeUnit;
45: import java.util.concurrent.atomic.AtomicBoolean;
46: 
47: /**
48:  *  LoggingAuditEventSender implementations that dequeue LoggingAuditEvent and send it to Kafka.
49:  *
50:  *  Each instance of this class should be executed in its own thread.
51:  */
52: public class AuditEventKafkaSender implements LoggingAuditEventSender {
53: 
54:   private static final Logger LOG = LoggerFactory.getLogger(AuditEventKafkaSender.class);
55: 
56:   private static final int MAX_RETRIES_FOR_SELECTION_RANDOM_PARTITION = 10;
57: 
58:   private static final int PARTITIONS_REFRESH_INTERVAL_IN_SECONDS = 30;
59: 
60:   /**
61:    *  When sender send audit events to Kafka,  it chooses a random partition and if it fails, it
62:    *  will choose another GOOD partition, this retry will happen at most numOfPartitionsToTrySending
63:    *  times before dropping the event. Note that, this is different from the retry handled by
64:    *  kafka client
65:    *  library when sending an event to a certain partition.
66:    */
67:   private static final int NUM_OF_PARTITIONS_TO_TRY_SENDING = 3;
68: 
69:   /**
70:    *  maximum time to wait when sender tries to dequeue events before returning null.
71:    */
72:   private static final int DEQUEUE_WAIT_IN_SECONDS = 30;
73: 
74: 
75:   /**
76:    *   when gracefully shutting down the Sender, the calling thread sleeps for some time and let
77:    *   the sender to send out audit events left in the queue if there are any.
78:    *   stopGracePeriodInSeconds is the maximum time reserved and
79:    *   stopGracePeriodInSeconds / THREAD_SLEEP_IN_SECONDS is the total rounds the calling thread
80:    *   will sleep.
81:    */
82:   private static final int THREAD_SLEEP_IN_SECONDS = 10;
83: 
84:   /**
85:    *  when gracefully shutting down the Sender, this field specifies maximum time for main thread
86:    *  to wait, in order to let the sender send out audit events left in the queue if there are any.
87:    */
88:   private int stopGracePeriodInSeconds = 300;
89: 
90: 
91:   /**
92:    * Logging audit stage, can be THRIFTLOGGER, SINGER, MERCED and so on
93:    */
94:   private final LoggingAuditStage stage;
95: 
96:   /**
97:    * host name
98:    */
99:   private final String host;
100: 
101:   /**
102:    *  LinkedBlockingDequeue to store the LoggingAuditEvents.  The max capacity is specified when
103:    *  creating this deque in the LoggingAuditClient.java
104:    *
105:    *  The AuditEventKafkaSender dequeue from the beginning of the deque, if does not send out event
106:    *  successfully, it will enqueue this event to the beginning of the queue.  Note that, if enqueue
107:    *  the failed event at the end of the queue, this event could be processed with quite some delay
108:    *  and this is not the behavior we want.  That's we use LinkedBlockingDequeue,
109:    *  not ArrayBlockingQueue.
110:    */
111:   private final LinkedBlockingDeque<LoggingAuditEvent> queue;
112: 
113:   /**
114:    * KafkaProducer instance to send audit events
115:    */
116:   private KafkaProducer<byte[], byte[]> kafkaProducer;
117: 
118:   /**
119:    * Serialize key and value to byte[]
120:    */
121:   private TSerializer serializer = new TSerializer();
122: 
123:   /**
124:    *  flag to control the start and stop of the executing thread.
125:    */
126:   private AtomicBoolean cancelled = new AtomicBoolean(false);
127: 
128:   /**
129:    *  topic to store the audit events
130:    */
131:   private String topic;
132: 
133:   /**
134:    *  name of this sender instance
135:    */
136:   private String name;
137: 
138:   /**
139:    *  executing thread
140:    */
141:   private Thread thread;
142: 
143:   /**
144:    *  List of PartitionInfo
145:    */
146:   private List<PartitionInfo> partitionInfoList = new ArrayList<>();
147: 
148:   /**
149:    *  last time when partition list was refreshed. we want to refresh partition list every 5 mins.
150:    */
151:   private long lastTimeUpdate = -1;
152: 
153:   /**
154:    *  If sending out to one partition fails, this partition is added to set;
155:    *  If sending out to one partition succeeds, this partition is removed if it was added before.
156:    */
157:   private Set<Integer> badPartitions = ConcurrentHashMap.newKeySet();
158: 
159:   /**
160:    * For each event (identified by LoggingAuditHeaders, key of the map), track the number of tries
161:    * for sending to Kafka. Each try will choose a different and partition that is not in the
162:    * badPartitions.  When event is send out successfully or dropped, the corresponding entry in
163:    * this map is removed.
164:    */
165: 
166:   private Map<LoggingAuditHeaders, Integer> eventTriedCount = new ConcurrentHashMap<>();
167: 
168:   /**
169:    *  currentPartitionId specifies the partition of audit_event topic used to receive audit events.
170:    *  The currentPartitionId will be reset in resetCurrentPartitionIdIfNeeded() method. This reduces
171:    *  the number of TCP connections from audit client to the Kafka Cluster hosting the audit_event
172:    *  topic.
173:    */
174:   private int currentPartitionId = -1;
175: 
176:   public AuditEventKafkaSender(KafkaSenderConfig config,
177:                                LinkedBlockingDeque<LoggingAuditEvent> queue,
178:                                LoggingAuditStage stage, String host, String name) {
179:     this.topic = config.getTopic();
180:     this.queue = queue;
181:     this.stage = stage;
182:     this.host = host;
183:     this.name = name;
184:     this.stopGracePeriodInSeconds = config.getStopGracePeriodInSeconds();
185:     this.badPartitions.add(-1);
186:   }
187: 
188: 
189:   public KafkaProducer<byte[], byte[]> getKafkaProducer() {
190:     return kafkaProducer;
191:   }
192: 
193:   public void setKafkaProducer(KafkaProducer<byte[], byte[]> kafkaProducer) {
194:     this.kafkaProducer = kafkaProducer;
195:   }
196: 
197:   private void refreshPartitionIfNeeded() {
198:     // refresh every 30 seconds
199:     if (System.currentTimeMillis() - lastTimeUpdate > 1000 * PARTITIONS_REFRESH_INTERVAL_IN_SECONDS) {
200:       try {
201:         badPartitions.clear();
202:         badPartitions.add(-1);
203:         partitionInfoList = this.kafkaProducer.partitionsFor(topic);
204:         lastTimeUpdate = System.currentTimeMillis();
205:         OpenTsdbMetricConverter.incr(
206:             LoggingAuditClientMetrics.AUDIT_CLIENT_SENDER_KAFKA_PARTITIONS_REFRESH_COUNT, 1,
207:                 "host=" + host, "stage=" + stage.toString());
208:       } catch (Exception e) {
209:         OpenTsdbMetricConverter.incr(
210:             LoggingAuditClientMetrics.AUDIT_CLIENT_SENDER_KAFKA_PARTITIONS_REFRESH_ERROR, 1,
211:                 "host=" + host, "stage=" + stage.toString());
212:       }
213:     }
214:     resetCurrentPartitionIdIfNeeded();
215:   }
216: 
217:   private void resetCurrentPartitionIdIfNeeded() {
218:     if (partitionInfoList.size() == 0) {
219:       currentPartitionId = -1;
220:       return;
221:     }
222:     if (badPartitions.contains(currentPartitionId)){
223:       int trial = 0;
224:       while (trial < MAX_RETRIES_FOR_SELECTION_RANDOM_PARTITION) {
225:         trial += 1;
226:         int index = ThreadLocalRandom.current().nextInt(partitionInfoList.size());
227:         int randomPartition = partitionInfoList.get(index).partition();
228:         if (!badPartitions.contains(randomPartition)) {
229:           LOG.warn("Change current partition of audit event topic from {} to {}", currentPartitionId,
230:               randomPartition);
231:           currentPartitionId = randomPartition;
232:           OpenTsdbMetricConverter.incr(
233:               LoggingAuditClientMetrics.AUDIT_CLIENT_SENDER_KAFKA_CURRENT_PARTITION_RESET, 1,
234:               "host=" + host, "stage=" + stage.toString());
235:           return;
236:         }
237:       }
238:       currentPartitionId =  partitionInfoList.get(ThreadLocalRandom.current().nextInt(
239:           partitionInfoList.size())).partition();
240:       LOG.warn("After {} trials, set current partition to {}",
241:           MAX_RETRIES_FOR_SELECTION_RANDOM_PARTITION, currentPartitionId);
242: 
243:     }
244:   }
245: 
246:   /**
247:    *  Sender dequeues LoggingAuditEvents and sends them to Kafka cluster. If send to one partition
248:    *  fails, it will choose another partition. For each event, it will try at most
249:    *  NUM_OF_PARTITIONS_TO_TRY_SENDING times (3 times) before dropping this event. Metrics are
250:    *  used to track the queue size and usuage, number of events sent out to Kafka successfully, and
251:    *  the number of events dropped.
252:    */
253:   @Override
254:   public void run() {
255:     LoggingAuditEvent event = null;
256:     ProducerRecord<byte[], byte[]> record;
257:     byte[] value = null;
258: 
259:     while (!cancelled.get()) {
260:       try {
261:         refreshPartitionIfNeeded();
262:         if (currentPartitionId == -1){
263:           Thread.sleep(100);
264:           continue;
265:         }
266:         event = queue.poll(DEQUEUE_WAIT_IN_SECONDS, TimeUnit.SECONDS);
267:         if (event != null) {
268:           try {
269:             value = serializer.serialize(event);
270:             record = new ProducerRecord<>(this.topic, currentPartitionId , null, value);
271:             kafkaProducer.send(record, new KafkaProducerCallback(event, currentPartitionId));
272:           } catch (TException e) {
273:             LOG.debug("[{}] failed to construct ProducerRecord because of serialization exception.",
274:                 Thread.currentThread().getName(), e);
275:             OpenTsdbMetricConverter
276:                 .incr(LoggingAuditClientMetrics.AUDIT_CLIENT_SENDER_SERIALIZATION_EXCEPTION, 1,
277:                     "host=" + host, "stage=" + stage.toString(),
278:                     "logName=" + event.getLoggingAuditHeaders().getLogName());
279:             eventTriedCount.remove(event.getLoggingAuditHeaders());
280:           }
281:         }
282:       } catch (InterruptedException e) {
283:         LOG.warn("[{}] got interrupted when polling the queue and while loop is ended!",
284:             Thread.currentThread().getName(), e);
285:         OpenTsdbMetricConverter.incr(
286:             LoggingAuditClientMetrics.AUDIT_CLIENT_SENDER_DEQUEUE_INTERRUPTED_EXCEPTION, 1,
287:                 "host=" + host, "stage=" + stage.toString());
288:         break;
289:       } catch (Exception e) {
290:         LOG.warn("Exit the while loop and finish the thread execution due to exception: ", e);
291:         OpenTsdbMetricConverter.incr(LoggingAuditClientMetrics.AUDIT_CLIENT_SENDER_EXCEPTION, 1,
292:             "host=" + host, "stage=" + stage.toString());
293:         break;
294:       }
295:     }
296:   }
297: 
298: 
299:   public class KafkaProducerCallback implements Callback {
300: 
301:     private LoggingAuditEvent event;
302:     private int partition;
303: 
304:     public KafkaProducerCallback(LoggingAuditEvent event, int partition) {
305:       this.event = event;
306:       this.partition = partition;
307:     }
308: 
309:     public void checkAndEnqueueWhenSendFailed() {
310:       // if exception thrown (i.e. the send failed), the partition is added to badPartitions.
311:       badPartitions.add(this.partition);
312:       OpenTsdbMetricConverter
313:           .incr(LoggingAuditClientMetrics.AUDIT_CLIENT_SENDER_KAFKA_PARTITION_ERROR, 1,
314:               "host=" + host, "stage=" + stage.toString(), "topic=" + topic,
315:               "partition=" + this.partition);
316: 
317:       // retry the failed event by inserting it at the beginning of the deque.
318:       // If number of tries reaches 3, meaning that 3 partitions have been tried sending to but
319:       // still failed, this event is dropped.
320:       Integer count = eventTriedCount.get(event.getLoggingAuditHeaders());
321:       if (count == null){
322:         eventTriedCount.put(event.getLoggingAuditHeaders(), 1);
323:         insertEvent(event);
324:         OpenTsdbMetricConverter
325:             .gauge(LoggingAuditClientMetrics.AUDIT_CLIENT_SENDER_KAFKA_EVENTS_RETRIED,
326:                 eventTriedCount.size(), "host=" + host, "stage=" + stage.toString(),
327:                 "topic=" + topic);
328:       } else if (count >= NUM_OF_PARTITIONS_TO_TRY_SENDING) {
329:           LOG.debug("Failed to send audit event after trying {} partitions. Drop event.", count);
330:           OpenTsdbMetricConverter
331:               .incr(LoggingAuditClientMetrics.AUDIT_CLIENT_SENDER_KAFKA_EVENTS_DROPPED, 1,
332:                   "host=" + host, "stage=" + stage.toString(),
333:                   "logName=" + event.getLoggingAuditHeaders().getLogName());
334:           eventTriedCount.remove(event.getLoggingAuditHeaders());
335:       } else {
336:           eventTriedCount.put(event.getLoggingAuditHeaders(), count + 1);
337:           insertEvent(event);
338:       }
339:     }
340: 
341:     public void insertEvent(LoggingAuditEvent event){
342:       try {
343:         boolean success = queue.offerFirst(event, 3, TimeUnit.SECONDS);
344:         if (!success) {
345:           LOG.debug("Failed to enqueue LoggingAuditEvent at head of the queue when executing "
346:               + "producer send callback. Drop this event.");
347:           eventTriedCount.remove(event.getLoggingAuditHeaders());
348:         }
349:       } catch (InterruptedException ex) {
350:         LOG.debug(
351:             "Enqueuing LoggingAuditEvent at head of the queue was interrupted in callback. "
352:                 + "Drop this event");
353:         eventTriedCount.remove(event.getLoggingAuditHeaders());
354:       }
355:     }
356: 
357:     @Override
358:     public void onCompletion(RecordMetadata recordMetadata, Exception e) {
359:       try {
360:         if (e == null) {
361:           OpenTsdbMetricConverter
362:               .incr(LoggingAuditClientMetrics.AUDIT_CLIENT_SENDER_KAFKA_EVENTS_ACKED, 1,
363:                   "host=" + host, "stage=" + stage.toString(),
364:                   "logName=" + event.getLoggingAuditHeaders().getLogName());
365: 
366:           // if send is successful, remove the event from the map eventTriedCount if it was added
367:           // LoggingAuditHeaders can uniquely identify an event.
368:           eventTriedCount.remove(event.getLoggingAuditHeaders());
369:           // if send out successfully, remove the partition from the badPartitions if it was added.
370:           badPartitions.remove(recordMetadata.partition());
371:         } else {
372:           checkAndEnqueueWhenSendFailed();
373:         }
374:       } catch (Throwable t) {
375:         LOG.warn("Exception throws in the callback. Drop this event {}", event, t);
376:         OpenTsdbMetricConverter
377:             .incr(LoggingAuditClientMetrics.AUDIT_CLIENT_SENDER_KAFKA_CALLBACK_EXCEPTION, 1,
378:                 "host=" + host, "stage=" + stage.toString(), "topic=" + topic);
379:       }
380:     }
381:   }
382: 
383:   /**
384:    *  start the executing thread and let the Sender run.
385:    */
386:   public synchronized void start() {
387:     if (this.thread == null) {
388:       thread = new Thread(this);
389:       thread.setDaemon(true);
390:       thread.setName(name);
391:       thread.start();
392:       LOG.warn(
393:           "[{}] created and started [{}] to let it dequeue LoggingAuditEvents and send to Kafka.",
394:           Thread.currentThread().getName(), name);
395:     }
396:   }
397: 
398:   /**
399:    *  reserve some time (by default 30 seconds at most)to let AuditEventKafkaSender to send out
400:    *  LoggingAuditEvent in the queue and gracefully stop AuditEventKafkaSender.
401:    */
402:   public synchronized void stop() {
403:     LOG.warn(
404:         "[{}] waits up to {} seconds to let [{}] send out LoggingAuditEvents left in the queue if"
405:             + " any.",
406:         Thread.currentThread().getName(), stopGracePeriodInSeconds, name);
407:     int i = 0;
408:     int numOfRounds = stopGracePeriodInSeconds / THREAD_SLEEP_IN_SECONDS;
409:     while (queue.size() > 0 && this.thread != null && thread.isAlive() && i < numOfRounds) {
410:       i += 1;
411:       try {
412:         Thread.sleep(THREAD_SLEEP_IN_SECONDS * 1000);
413:         CommonUtils.reportQueueUsage(queue.size(), queue.remainingCapacity(), host, stage.toString());
414:         LOG.info("In {} round, [{}] waited {} seconds and the current queue size is {}", i,
415:             Thread.currentThread().getName(), THREAD_SLEEP_IN_SECONDS, queue.size());
416:       } catch (InterruptedException e) {
417:         LOG.warn("[{}] got interrupted while waiting for [{}] to send out LoggingAuditEvents left "
418:             + "in the queue.", Thread.currentThread().getName(), name, e);
419:       }
420:     }
421:     cancelled.set(true);
422:     if (this.thread != null && thread.isAlive()) {
423:       thread.interrupt();
424:     }
425:     try {
426:       this.kafkaProducer.close();
427:     } catch (Throwable t) {
428:       LOG.warn("Exception is thrown while stopping {}.", name, t);
429:     }
430:     LOG.warn("[{}] is stopped and the number of LoggingAuditEvents left in the queue is {}.", name,
431:         queue.size());
432:   }
433: 
434: }

(End of file - total 434 lines)

   - Removed the initializer from `private TSerializer serializer = new TSerializer();`
   - Added a static initializer block with try-catch for `TTransportException`
